title: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick — Page 347
raw_text:
I have seen the Savior wrapped in the crazed, crapping, dirty, wild body
of an animal, then transformed and eternally my friend. Christ in deliberate
disguise, and the passion fulfilled in victory: resurrection. Only when I felt his
hand on my shoulder and dreamed the "Donlevy" dreams and had the vision
of him thrusting himself through the 3:5 door, renewed and strong, did I
understand (as with the disciples—only afterward did they know who had
been with them).
work_title: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick
